Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack messages with a query related to "servicing transfer" or "LN-2025-00509" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Keith's own message in #loan-processing (channel C002) from April 28, 2026, which reads: "Heads up on LN-2025-00509 -- servicing got transferred over post-close. I noted it in the file in case borrower calls on payment routing." This identifies the loan number and confirms it is Amy Chen's closed file.
OE 2: Pull the loan details for LN-2025-00509 using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2025-00509") to discover: borrower Abigail Foster, loan officer Amy Chen (los_staff_4d01ebef3a15), status closed, closing date 2026-01-19, FHA loan for $221,000, title company Piedmont Settlement Services, property at 5332 Sharon Rd, Charlotte, NC 28205.
OE 3: Check the document checklist for LN-2025-00509 using mortgage_los_get_documents (loan_id: "los_loan_9d857160b1f9") to review whether a servicing transfer notice is on file or any documents are flagged.
OE 4: Search emails with a query related to "servicing transfer" or "LN-2025-00509" or "Abigail Foster" or "transfer notice" (or similar) using search_emails to look for a formal transfer notice or any correspondence about when the transfer went effective. On April 28, 2026 (today), no formal transfer notice email is found in the searchable inbox for this date. The agent should note the absence.
OE 5: Add a detailed activity note to LN-2025-00509 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_9d857160b1f9", action: "note", detail: a summary noting the servicing transfer was flagged post-close, no formal transfer notice document was located in email or on file, and follow-up with the lender is needed to obtain the notice and confirm the effective transfer date).
OE 6: Search Slack messages with a query related to "Raymond" or "spot check" or "exception" or "conditions cleared" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Raymond Chen's messages in #loan-processing (channel C002) from late March 28 to early March 29, 2026: (1) on March 28 at 23:55 -- "Heads up, during post-close spot check I found 3 files where conds show cleared in LOS but I'm not seeing the supporting PDFs in the doc folders. One is already closed. Pulling notes now." and (2) on March 29 at 00:43 - "Grace, just emailed you the QC summary. 3 Tyler-cleared files w missing support docs, incl LN-2026-00603 which already closed."
OE 7: Search emails with a query related to "Raymond" or "QC exceptions" or "Tyler-cleared" or "spot check" or "LN-2026-00603" (or similar) using search_emails to find Raymond's detailed email to Grace Yamamoto (subject: "QC exceptions found on 3 Tyler-cleared files") identifying all three flagged loans: LN-2026-00603 (listed as "Heather Sullivan - closed" in the email), LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00224, with details that Tyler Washington cleared conditions without supporting docs across all three files.
OE 8: Pull the loan details for LN-2026-00603 using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00603") to discover: borrower Janet Torres, status processing, title company Tryon Title & Escrow LLC (note: Raymond's email names the borrower as "Heather Sullivan" and claims status "closed", but LOS returns borrower "Janet Torres" and status "processing" - both are data discrepancies the agent may encounter).
OE 9: Pull the loan details for LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00211") to discover: borrower Elizabeth Gray, status withdrawn, title company Tryon Title & Escrow LLC (note: Raymond's email says "The other two files are still open" but LOS returns status "withdrawn" - this is a data discrepancy the agent may encounter).
OE 10: Pull the loan details for LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00224") to discover: borrower Steven Thomas, status closed (closing date 2026-03-10), title company Tryon Title & Escrow LLC (note: Raymond's email says "The other two files are still open" but LOS returns status "closed" -- this is a data discrepancy the agent may encounter).
OE 11: Check the document checklist for LN-2026-00603 using mortgage_los_get_documents (loan_id: "los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f") to review which documents are present versus missing, and compare against Raymond's finding that conditions were cleared without supporting PDFs.
OE 12: Check the document checklist for LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_get_documents (loan_id: "los_loan_223826af9a5d") to review which documents are present versus missing, and compare against Raymond's finding.
OE 13: Check the document checklist for LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_get_documents (loan_id: "los_loan_48275b0214f9") to review which documents are present versus missing, and compare against Raymond's finding.
OE 14: Add an activity note to LN-2026-00603 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f", action: "note", detail: a summary noting Raymond's spot check found conditions cleared by Tyler Washington without supporting documentation on file, listing which documents are missing or unverified based on the document checklist review).
OE 15: Add an activity note to LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_223826af9a5d", action: "note", detail: similar findings from Raymond's spot check for this loan).
OE 16: Add an activity note to LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_48275b0214f9", action: "note", detail: similar findings from Raymond's spot check for this loan).
OE 17: Post an update message to #loan-processing using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload: a summary covering both issues - the servicing transfer on Amy's file LN-2025-00509 with no formal transfer notice found, and the document gap findings from Raymond's spot check across the three flagged loans, including whether any missing docs are from the title company).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 A loan activity note was added (via mortgage_los_add_activity) to loan LN-2025-00509 (loan_id: "los_loan_9d857160b1f9", borrower Abigail Foster) and the tool returned success.
outcome

2 The activity note on LN-2025-00509 (visible in the mortgage_los_add_activity detail parameter) mentions that a servicing transfer was identified post-close (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The activity note detail clearly states: 'Servicing on this closed FHA file (closed 01/19/2026, Caliber Home Loans) has transferred to a new servicer,' confirming the servicing transfer post-close is documented.
✓Run #2The activity note detail explicitly states 'Borrower reports 30-day late credit hit after servicing transfer post-close' in the SUMMARY section, clearly documenting the servicing transfer finding.
✓Run #3The activity note detail explicitly states 'Servicing on this loan was transferred post-close from Caliber Home Loans (original lender).' This clearly identifies the servicing transfer as the core finding.
✓Run #4The detail parameter of the activity note on LN-2025-00509 explicitly states: 'SUMMARY: Servicing transfer confirmed on this closed FHA purchase (closed 01/19/2026, Caliber Home Loans). Borrower's loan has been transferred away from original servicer.'
✓Run #5The activity note detail states 'Servicing on this closed FHA loan was transferred from Homestead Loan Servicing to a new servicer post-close,' clearly documenting the servicing transfer.
✓Run #6The activity note detail explicitly states 'Servicing on this closed FHA purchase was transferred post-close from the original servicer to a new servicer.' The servicing transfer is clearly documented.
3 The activity note on LN-2025-00509 (visible in the mortgage_los_add_activity detail parameter) states that no formal servicing transfer notice was found in email or on file (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The activity note explicitly states: 'NO formal servicing transfer notice (Hello/Goodbye letter) found in the loan file, filesystem document folder, or email system,' directly flagging the absence of the transfer notice.
✓Run #2The activity note includes a 'TRANSFER NOTICE STATUS — NO FORMAL NOTICE ON FILE' section stating 'No goodbye letter or hello letter found in loan document folder' and 'No transfer-related correspondence found in email system.'
✓Run #3The activity note explicitly states 'NO formal servicing transfer notice found in LOS document checklist, email system, or loan file folder' and further notes that no LN-2025-00509 folder exists in the document filesystem.
✓Run #4The detail of the activity note states: 'NO FORMAL SERVICING TRANSFER NOTICE FOUND ON FILE. Searched email (inbox/sent), filesystem document folders, and LOS attachments. No goodbye letter from Homestead, no hello letter from new servicer, and no copy of the RESPA-required transfer notice is in our records.'
✓Run #5The activity note explicitly states 'TRANSFER NOTICE STATUS: NO formal servicing transfer notice found in Keystone email, filesystem, or LOS document checklist.'
✓Run #6The activity note detail explicitly states 'TRANSFER NOTICE STATUS: NO FORMAL TRANSFER NOTICE ON FILE. Checked LOS document checklist, filesystem (no doc folder for LN-2025-00509), and email — no copy of servicing transfer notice from either old or new servicer is in our records.'
4 The activity note on LN-2025-00509 (visible in the mortgage_los_add_activity detail parameter) mentions that follow-up with the lender is needed to obtain the transfer notice or confirm the effective transfer date (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The activity note's ACTION NEEDED section item #1 states: 'Contact Caliber Home Loans to obtain copies of both the old servicer's Goodbye letter and the new servicer's Hello letter, including effective transfer date and new payment address,' which is a clear follow-up with the lender directive.
✓Run #2The activity note ACTION NEEDED section item 1 says 'Contact Caliber Home Loans — obtain goodbye letter, confirm effective transfer date' and item 5 says 'CRITICAL: No transfer notice documentation on file — follow up with Caliber immediately.'
✓Run #3The activity note includes action item '1. Contact Caliber Home Loans to obtain copy of the Goodbye Letter and confirm effective transfer date and new servicer identity.' It also flags 'Keith needs to follow up with Caliber to close this gap.'
✓Run #4The note's ACTION NEEDED section includes: 'Contact Caliber Home Loans to obtain copies of both the goodbye letter (old servicer) and hello letter (new servicer) with effective transfer date' and 'Flag for Denise if lender does not provide within 48 hours.'
✓Run #5The activity note includes 'ACTION NEEDED: Follow up with Caliber Home Loans (lender) to obtain: (1) goodbye letter from Homestead with effective transfer date...' clearly flagging lender follow-up.
✓Run #6The activity note ACTION NEEDED section states: '(1) Follow up with Caliber Home Loans for formal transfer notice, effective date, old/new servicer details.' This clearly flags lender follow-up.

6 The activity note on LN-2026-00603 (visible in the mortgage_los_add_activity detail parameter) mentions that conditions were cleared without supporting documentation, or references Raymond's QC spot check findings (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The activity note on LN-2026-00603 explicitly references 'Raymond Chen (QC Analyst) flagged this file during post-close spot check as one of 3 Tyler Washington-cleared files where conditions show cleared in the LOS but supporting PDFs are not present in the document folders.'
